engineGuide.h2: Mercedes Benz Viano Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Mercedes Benz Viano across all generations (W638 1996-2003, W639 2003-2014), with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.2L Diesel
entries.history: The OM611DE22LA was Mercedes' first common-rail diesel in the Viano/W638 platform, replacing older indirect injection units with improved efficiency and driveability.
compatibleVariants: 2.2 CDI (W638), Viano 2.2 CDI pre-facelift, Viano 108 CDI, Viano 110 CDI
commonFailures: Injector pump seal failure - fuel leaks into oil causing dilution and bearing damage (OEM A6110700107)
Glow plug relay thermal failure - hard starting, especially in cold weather
Crankcase breather oil carryover - oil mist enters intake causing carbon buildup
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM611 Mercedes Benz Viano engine replacement

entries.title: 2.7L Diesel
entries.history: A rare but documented Viano diesel option in select W638 markets, the OM612 shares the injection pump design flaws of the OM611 family with slightly larger displacement.
commonFailures: Injector pump seal degradation - identical failure pattern to OM611
Glow plug module failure - hard start conditions
EGR valve carbon buildup
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM612 Mercedes Benz Viano engine replacement

entries.title: 2.2L Diesel
entries.history: The OM646 powers the majority of W639 Viano diesels (2003-2010), featuring common-rail injection with power outputs from 109hp to 150hp across the 109 CDI, 111 CDI, and 115 CDI variants.
compatibleVariants: 109 CDI, 111 CDI, 115 CDI, Viano 2.2 CDI, Viano Ambiante CDI, Viano Trend CDI, 2.0 CDI (detuned OM646)
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner failure (OEM A6460500307) - chain rattle and potential catastrophic engine failure; updated dual-spring tensioner required
EGR cooler carbon lock - Euro IV models (2006-2010) suffer internal coking from short-trip driving
Glow plug relay failure (OEM A0035451107) - hard start and engine management light
Crankshaft/camshaft correlation codes - often indicates chain stretch or tensioner failure
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM646 Mercedes Benz Viano engine replacement

entries.title: 2.2L Diesel
entries.history: Unveiled in 2008 and fitted to facelift W639 Viano models from 2010, the OM651 brought improved fuel economy and reduced emissions but introduced a new set of reliability concerns around chain stretch and fuel system durability.
compatibleVariants: 113 CDI (136hp), 116 CDI (163hp), Viano 2.2 CDI facelift, Viano Ambiante CDI, Viano Trend CDI
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch - roller chain design elongates causing rattle at rear of engine; technically complex and costly replacement
Fuel pump failure (TSB 07.01.15) - fails without warning, common in high-mileage fleets
EGR cooler cracks under thermal stress - leads to coolant loss
Turbo actuator clogging - especially in stop-start urban use
DPF blockage - frequent in short-trip driving patterns
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM651 Mercedes Benz Viano engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L V6 Diesel
entries.history: The OM642 3.0-litre V6 turbodiesel was Mercedes' premium diesel option for the facelift W639 Viano (2010-2014), offering 204hp and significantly more torque than four-cylinder variants.
compatibleVariants: 3.0 CDI, Viano 3.0 CDI, Viano Ambiante 3.0 CDI
commonFailures: Oil cooler seal failure - leads to oil leaks onto belt tensioner and accessory drive
Turbo actuator failure - stuck vanes due to carbon buildup
EGR cooler failure - cracks under thermal load
entries.cta: Get quotes for OM642 Mercedes Benz Viano engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L / 2.3L Petrol
entries.history: The M111 four-cylinder petrol engine powered base-spec W638 Viano models in markets where diesel was less dominant, though UK take-up was minimal compared to diesel variants.
commonFailures: Head gasket failure - coolant-oil mixing common after overheating events
Timing chain rattle - tensioner wear at higher mileage
Oil leaks from valve cover gaskets
entries.cta: Get quotes for M111 Mercedes Benz Viano engine replacement

entries.title: 3.2L / 3.7L V6 Petrol
entries.history: The M112 V6 represented the premium petrol option in W639 Viano models, offering smooth power delivery at the cost of significant fuel consumption. The 3.7-litre variant is rare in UK specification.
compatibleVariants: 3.2 V6, 3.7 V6, Viano 3.2, Viano 3.7, Viano Ambiante V6, Viano Trend V6
commonFailures: Head gasket degradation - composite gasket design prone to coolant-oil mixing; MLS gasket recommended for replacement
Oil leaks from camshaft plugs (30mm and 65mm) and oil cooler seals
Thermostat failure - map-controlled unit fails, causing temperature fluctuations
entries.cta: Get quotes for M112 Mercedes Benz Viano engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L / 3.5L V6 Petrol
entries.history: The M272 represented Mercedes' next-generation V6 with four valves per cylinder, continuously adjustable variable valve timing, and balance shafts - but early production units (2004-2008) suffered a catastrophic design flaw.
compatibleVariants: 3.0 V6, 3.5 V6, Viano 3.5, Viano Ambiante V6 (facelift)
commonFailures: Balance shaft gear wear (2004-2008 production) - balance shaft sprocket erodes from timing chain contact, causing engine to fall out of time; fault codes P0059, P0060, P0064; engine-out service required
Timing chain tensioner failure - shares M272 family flaws regardless of displacement
Oil cooler seal leaks - oil mist contaminates belt tensioner
Camshaft adjuster magnet leaks - oil wicks up engine harness
entries.cta: Get quotes for M272 Mercedes Benz Viano engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Mercedes Benz Viano Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.group: Mercedes Viano OM646 Timing Chain Tensioner Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Viano OM646 Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - The Most Common Diesel Engine Killer
problems.rootCause: The original OM646 timing chain tensioner (OEM A6460500307) fails to maintain adequate oil pressure at idle, allowing the single-row timing chain to slacken. This causes chain rattle, timing correlation faults, and eventual chain skip - leading to piston-valve contact and catastrophic engine failure .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement of timing chain, updated dual-spring tensioner (A6460502201), guides, and sprockets
repairOptions.longevity: 80k-100k miles; only viable if caught before chain slip occurs

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of reconditioned OM646 with upgraded tensioner, reinforced guide rails, and 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: 100k+ miles; the definitive fix for engines with timing damage or skipped chains

problems.recommendation: If you hear chain rattle on cold start or have camshaft correlation fault codes (P0016/P0017), act immediately. Preventative chain replacement at a specialist (£768-£1,280) is far cheaper than a full engine replacement. On a Viano worth £3,000-£6,000, once the chain has slipped and damaged valves, replacement is usually the correct financial decision.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt OM646 engine prices for your Mercedes Viano

problems.group: Mercedes Viano M272 Balance Shaft Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Viano M272 Balance Shaft Failure - The V6 Petrol Catastrophe
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Viano 3.0 V6, 3.5 V6 (2005-2008, M272.940, M272.960 - early production engines only)
problems.rootCause: A manufacturing defect in early M272 engines used soft metal for the balance shaft sprocket. The sprocket teeth wear prematurely, causing the timing chain to slacken and camshaft timing to drift. Fault codes P0016/P0017/P0018/P0019 confirm the issue .

repairOptions.tier: Full Repair - Balance Shaft & Timing Components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine-out replacement of balance shaft with updated hardened version, plus timing chain, guides, and tensioners
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent fix with updated parts; requires 20-30 labour hours

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of reconditioned M272 (post-2008 specification with hardened balance shaft)
repairOptions.longevity: The preferred option if engine has suffered valve damage or if vehicle has high mileage

problems.recommendation: On a Viano V6, repair costs often exceed the vehicle's value. However, if the van is otherwise excellent, the updated balance shaft permanently resolves the issue. For high-mileage examples (120k+), replacement with a post-2008 rebuilt unit is often more cost-effective than repairing a worn engine. Check your engine number - not all M272 engines are affected .
problems.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Viano M272 V6 engine replacement

problems.group: Mercedes Viano OM642 Oil Cooler Seal Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Viano OM642 Oil Cooler Seal Failure - The Costly V6 Diesel Leak
problems.rootCause: The oil cooler sits deep in the "vee" of the OM642 V6 engine. The rubber seals between the cooler and engine block degrade over time, causing oil to leak down the back of the engine and onto the driveway. The seals themselves cost under £10 - but accessing them requires extensive disassembly .

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement of oil cooler seals, intake manifold gaskets, and related seals while accessible
repairOptions.longevity: 80k-100k miles; engine remains sound after repair

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of reconditioned OM642 with new seals, upgraded components, and warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only necessary if oil starvation has caused bearing damage

problems.recommendation: This is an expensive job for what seems like a minor leak - but it's a known, predictable failure on the OM642. Don't ignore it; oil loss can lead to belt contamination and eventual engine seizure. Use a reputable Mercedes specialist (£800-£1,400) rather than a dealer. If caught early with no engine damage, the seal replacement is the correct fix .
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt OM642 engine prices for your Viano 3.0 CDI

problems.group: Mercedes Viano OM611 / OM612 Injector Pump Seal Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes Viano OM611 / OM612 Injector Pump Seal Failure - Fuel in Oil Disaster
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Viano 2.2 CDI, 2.7 CDI (W638 generation, 1996-2003, OM611, OM612)
problems.rootCause: The injector pump seal fails internally, allowing diesel fuel to leak into the engine oil sump. The rising oil level and thinned, fuel-diluted oil causes rapid bearing wear, low oil pressure, and eventual catastrophic engine failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement of injector pump seals, full oil and filter change (multiple flushes may be required)
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent fix if caught before bearing damage occurs

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit of reconditioned engine with new seals, pump, and bearings
repairOptions.longevity: Required if engine has run with fuel-diluted oil for extended period

problems.recommendation: Check your dipstick regularly - if the oil level rises between services or smells strongly of diesel, stop driving immediately. Early detection means a £350-£600 specialist repair. Ignoring it will destroy the bottom end and require a full replacement engine. On an older W638 Viano, replacement often exceeds the vehicle's value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for OM611 / OM612 Mercedes Viano replacement engines

problems.group: Mercedes Viano EGR Cooler Carbon Lock
problems.h4: Mercedes Viano EGR Cooler Carbon Lock - The Euro IV Diesel Killer
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes Viano 109 CDI, 111 CDI, 115 CDI (2006-2010, OM646 Euro IV emissions specification)
problems.rootCause: The EGR cooler on Euro IV compliant OM646 engines becomes blocked with hard carbon deposits from repeated short-trip driving and inadequate regeneration cycles. This leads to restricted exhaust flow, EGR valve sticking, loss of power, and eventual cooler failure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replacement of EGR cooler, EGR valve cleaning or replacement, and DPF regeneration
Supply & fit of reconditioned OM646 with cleaned or deleted EGR system and updated cooling
repairOptions.longevity: Best for vehicles used primarily for short trips

problems.recommendation: If your Viano is used for school runs or local shuttling, the EGR cooler will block again. Consider a professional EGR delete (where MOT-legal) or switch to a specialist who can fit a modified cooling setup. The replacement is straightforward - but the root cause is driving pattern.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt OM646 engine prices for your Viano CDI

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mercedes Viano
finalCta.paragraph: From OM646 timing chain tensioners to M272 balance shaft failures, the Viano has several known engine weaknesses - but all can be addressed. Our network of UK Mercedes Viano specialists offers rebuilt engines with upgraded components designed to eliminate the original design flaws. Every replacement engine comes with a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty, nationwide delivery, and supply & fit options. Compare prices today - don't let a failed engine send your Viano to the scrapyard.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mercedes Viano Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mercedes Viano and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Mercedes Benz Viano Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Mercedes Viano across W638 (1996-2003) and W639 (2003-2014) generations is listed below with compatible engine codes, years and common failure notes.

groups.title: Mercedes Benz Viano Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz Viano 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre diesel was a lower-power entry point for the W639 Viano, primarily in European markets with favourable tax treatment. UK uptake was limited, but the engine shares OM646 architecture with the more common 2.2-litre units. It offers genuine economy gains for private hire operators running urban duty cycles.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner weakness (shared with 2.2 OM646 family)
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz Viano 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz Viano 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: The backbone of the UK Viano diesel range across both W638 and W639 generations. This displacement covers three distinct engine families - OM611 (early W638), OM646 (W639 pre-facelift, 2003-2010) and OM651 (W639 facelift, 2010-2014). It powered the majority of private hire Vianos and remains the most frequently replaced diesel unit in the UK.
commonFailurePoints: OM646 timing chain tensioner failure; OM651 timing chain stretch; OM611 injector pump seal failure
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz Viano 2.2L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz Viano 2.7L Diesel Engine
items.description: A rare diesel option in the first-generation W638 Viano (1996-2003), the 2.7-litre OM612 shares the injection pump seal vulnerability of the smaller OM611 but offers additional torque for heavier passenger or load duties. Fewer than 15% of W638 UK imports carried this engine, but replacement demand persists among owners of high-spec import vans.
commonFailurePoints: Injector pump seal failure causing diesel contamination in engine oil
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz Viano 2.7L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz Viano 3.0L Diesel Engine
items.description: The OM642 3.0-litre V6 turbodiesel was introduced with the W639 facelift (2010-2014) as a premium alternative to the four-cylinder OM651. It delivers 204hp and significantly higher torque, making it the preferred choice for luxury people carrier conversions and high-spec Ambiante models. However, the OM642's complexity drives higher replacement costs.
commonFailurePoints: Oil cooler seal failure (deep-vee engine design); turbo actuator carbon sticking
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz Viano 3.0L diesel engines

groups.title: Mercedes Benz Viano Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes Benz Viano 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: A rarity on UK roads, the M111 2.0-litre petrol four-cylinder powered base-spec W638 Viano models primarily sold into continental European markets where diesel penetration was lower. UK imports exist but represent less than 2% of the Viano population. Replacement engines are typically sourced from European breakers.
commonFailurePoints: Head gasket failure; timing chain rattle at higher mileages
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz Viano 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz Viano 3.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The M112 3.2-litre V6 was the premium petrol option for early W639 Viano models (2003-2008), offering smooth power delivery and refinement unmatched by the diesel range. UK take-up was limited due to high fuel consumption and road tax costs, but enthusiasts and chauffeur operators valued the quiet cabin experience.
commonFailurePoints: rocker cover gaskets; head gasket degradation
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz Viano 3.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz Viano 3.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The M272 3.5-litre V6 replaced the 3.2-litre in facelift W639 Viano models (2008-2014), bringing increased power (231hp) and variable valve timing. However, early production units (2004-2008) carry the infamous balance shaft defect - a catastrophic failure requiring engine removal. Post-2008 examples are significantly more reliable.
commonFailurePoints: Balance shaft sprocket wear (2004-2008 engines); timing chain tensioner failure
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz Viano 3.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz Viano 3.7L Petrol Engines
items.description: The largest displacement ever fitted to the Viano, the M112 3.7-litre V6 delivered 235hp and was offered as a very low-volume special-order option in the W639 range (2005-2010). Fewer than 200 examples are believed to have reached UK shores. Replacement engines are scarce and command a specialist premium. Not sure which engine size your Mercedes Benz Viano needs? Enter your registration number above - we'll identify the exact displacement, engine code and compatible replacement options instantly. All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK Mercedes Benz Viano specialists.
commonFailurePoints: Oil cooler seal failure; timing chain tensioner wear; high fuel consumption
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz Viano 3.7L petrol engine prices

fuelTypes.tag: Mercedes Benz Viano Engines Fuel Type
fuelTypes.h2: Mercedes Benz Viano Engines by Fuel Type

items.title: Mercedes Benz Viano Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel dominates UK Viano replacement enquiries, spanning three generations of 2.2-litre four-cylinder units (OM611, OM646, OM651) plus the rare 2.7 OM612 and 3.0 OM642 V6.
items.descriptor: Diesel dominates UK Viano replacement enquiries, spanning three generations of 2.2-litre four-cylinder units (OM611, OM646, OM651) plus the rare 2.7 OM612 and 3.0 OM642 V6.
items.familiesLabel: Common Mercedes Benz Viano Diesel Engine Families
families: OM611 - early W638 2.2 CDI (90-125hp), injector pump seal failure common
OM646 - W639 pre-facelift 2.2 CDI (109-150hp), timing chain tensioner the main failure point
OM651 - W639 facelift 2.2 CDI (136-163hp), chain stretch and fuel pump failures
OM642 - W639 facelift 3.0 V6 CDI (204hp), oil cooler seal leaks in the V6
foundIn: W639 pre-facelift (2003-2010) - OM646 2.2 CDI (109 CDI, 111 CDI, 115 CDI)
W639 facelift (2010-2014) - OM651 2.2 CDI (113 CDI, 116 CDI), OM642 3.0 CDI
knownFor: Excellent motorway fuel economy (especially OM651 2.2 CDI)
OM646 timing chain tensioner failures between 80k-140k miles
Highest UK replacement demand of any Viano fuel type (over 85% of enquiries)
OM651 timing chain stretch requiring engine-out replacement on facelift models
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Benz Viano Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Mercedes Benz Viano Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Vianos are rare in the UK (under 10% of the market) but carry higher average order values due to V6 complexity and scarcity - spanning M111 four-cylinder and M112/M272 V6 units.
items.descriptor: Petrol Vianos are rare in the UK (under 10% of the market) but carry higher average order values due to V6 complexity and scarcity - spanning M111 four-cylinder and M112/M272 V6 units.
items.familiesLabel: Common Mercedes Benz Viano Petrol Engine Families
families: M111 - W638 2.0L four-cylinder (129hp), rare UK import only
M112 - W639 3.2L and 3.7L V6 (190-235hp), smooth but thirsty
M272 - W639 facelift 3.5L V6 (231-258hp), balance shaft failure risk on pre-2008 units
knownFor: Refined V6 power delivery - quieter than any diesel Viano
Very low UK volume - replacement engines harder to source
M272 balance shaft failure (2004-2008 production) catastrophic and expensive
High fuel consumption (20-25mpg real world on V6 models)
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Benz Viano Petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes Benz Viano 4MATIC (All-Wheel Drive) Variants
items.description: 4MATIC was an option across both diesel and petrol Viano engines, adding a permanent all-wheel-drive system without changing the underlying engine specification.
items.descriptor: 4MATIC was an option across both diesel and petrol Viano engines, adding a permanent all-wheel-drive system without changing the underlying engine specification.
families: Permanent all-wheel drive with 45/55 rear bias split
Transfer case integrated with automatic transmission
Same engine failure patterns as 2WD variants - only transfer case adds complexity
importantNotes: 4MATIC does not change the engine replacement cost - same prices as 2WD
Transfer case should be inspected during engine replacement on high-mileage units
4MATIC Vianos are less common - allow extra time for specialist sourcing
Engine removal procedure differs slightly; ensure your specialist has 4MATIC experience

engineYears.h2: Mercedes Benz Viano Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section maps every major engine change across the Mercedes Viano production timeline (W638 1996-2003, W639 2003-2014), helping owners identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original Viano (badged as V-Class in some markets) launched with OM611 common-rail diesel and M111 petrol engines - establishing the MPV format Mercedes would refine for another two decades.
keyChanges: OM611 common-rail diesel introduced - Mercedes' first in an MPV
M111 petrol four-cylinder available for non-UK markets
Rare OM612 2.7-litre diesel added later in production
knownFor: OM611 injector pump seal failure - diesel contamination of engine oil is a known killer
W638 rust issues around wheel arches and sliding door tracks
Simple electronics make these older Vianos easier and cheaper to repair than W639
Low UK survival rate - most W638 Vianos have exceeded 150k miles or been scrapped
years.cta: Get quotes for 1996-2003 Mercedes Viano W638 replacement engines
years.ticker: Mercedes Viano 2.2 CDI engine replacement • OM611 injector pump seal failure • W638 Viano engine seized due to oil dilution
enquiries: W638 Viano engine seized due to oil dilution

years.preview: W639 Launch: OM646 Diesel & M112 Petrol Arrive
years.description: The W639 Viano launched with OM646 2.2 CDI four-cylinder diesel and M112 3.2 V6 petrol - bringing modern common-rail refinement and the timing chain tensioner weakness that would define the generation.
keyChanges: W639 platform debut - redesigned body and chassis
OM646 diesel replaces OM611 - Euro III emissions initially
M112 3.2 V6 petrol introduced as premium option
109 CDI, 111 CDI, 115 CDI power outputs established
Early 4MATIC all-wheel drive option available from 2005
mainEngines: M112 - 3.7 V6 petrol (235hp) - Viano 3.7 V6 (special order from 2005)
knownFor: OM646 timing chain tensioner failure (A6460500307) - rattle on cold start is the warning sign
Single-row chain design was under-specced; updated dual-spring tensioner is the permanent fix
M112 V6 is smooth but thirsty (20-25mpg real world)
109 CDI detuned OM646 - same chain vulnerability as higher-power variants
years.cta: Compare 2003-2006 Mercedes Viano W639 engine prices
years.ticker: Mercedes Viano 115 CDI engine replacement • OM646 timing chain tensioner failure • Viano 2.2 CDI chain rattle fix • Viano 3.2 V6 engine for sale

years.preview: W639 Pre-Facelift: Euro IV OM646 & M272 Petrol
years.description: Euro IV emissions compliance brought EGR coolers to the OM646 diesel - and with them, carbon lock failures. The M272 3.5 V6 petrol replaced the M112 3.2, introducing balance shaft vulnerability on early production units.
keyChanges: Euro IV OM646 gains EGR cooler - carbon lock becomes a common failure
M272 3.5 V6 petrol replaces M112 3.2 - variable valve timing added
DPF introduced on diesel variants (Euro IV requirement)
4MATIC option continues across diesel and petrol ranges
Production years affected by M272 balance shaft defect (2004-2008 engines)
mainEngines: OM646 (Euro IV) - 2.2 diesel (109-150hp) - 109 CDI, 111 CDI, 115 CDI
M112 - 3.7 V6 petrol (235hp) - Viano 3.7 V6 (limited availability)
knownFor: EGR cooler carbon lock on short-trip urban Vianos - loss of power, limp mode, eventual cooler failure
M272 balance shaft sprocket wear on pre-2008 engines - engine-out repair, often uneconomical
DPF regeneration issues on diesel models used primarily for school runs or local shuttling
115 CDI (150hp) is the most searched Viano diesel replacement query
years.cta: Get quotes for 2006-2010 Mercedes Viano W639 replacement engines
years.ticker: Mercedes Viano 115 CDI EGR cooler failure • OM646 timing chain tensioner replacement • Viano 3.5 V6 balance shaft failure repair cost • Viano 2.2 CDI DPF blockage fix
enquiries: Mercedes Viano 115 CDI EGR cooler failure
Viano 3.5 V6 balance shaft failure repair cost

years.preview: W639 Facelift: OM651 Diesel & 3.0 V6 CDI Arrive
years.description: The 2010 facelift Viano brought OM651 twin-turbo diesel with 136-163hp outputs, plus the OM642 3.0 V6 CDI as a premium option - but introduced timing chain stretch and fuel pump failures to the diesel range.
keyChanges: OM642 3.0 V6 CDI added as flagship diesel option (204hp)
M272 3.5 V6 petrol continues for petrol buyers (231hp)
113 CDI (136hp) and 116 CDI (163hp) replace old 109/111/115 badges
Stop-start system introduced on diesel variants
knownFor: OM651 timing chain stretch (rear-mounted) - technically complex and costly engine-out replacement
OM642 oil cooler seal failure - deep-vee engine design makes a £10 seal into a £800+ labour job
116 CDI (163hp) is the most sought-after facelift diesel variant
OM651 fuel pump failure (TSB 07.01.15) - fails without warning on high-mileage fleet Vianos
Final Viano production year (2014) - W639 replaced by Mercedes V-Class
years.cta: Compare 2010-2014 Mercedes Viano W639 facelift engine prices
years.ticker: Mercedes Viano 116 CDI engine replacement • OM651 timing chain stretch repair • Viano 3.0 CDI oil cooler seal failure • Viano 113 CDI fuel pump failure • OM642 engine replacement cost
enquiries: Viano 3.0 CDI oil cooler seal failure

faq.h2: Mercedes Benz Viano Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: What is the common failure on the Mercedes Viano OM646 engine?
items.answer: The OM646 timing chain tensioner (A6460500307) fails between 80k-140k miles, causing chain rattle and potential catastrophic engine failure if ignored.
keyPoints: Cold start rattle is the earliest warning sign of tensioner failure
Updated dual-spring tensioner (A6460502201) is the permanent fix
Catch it early - preventative chain replacement costs £768-£1,280 at a specialist
Once the chain slips, piston-valve contact usually means a full replacement engine
Most common on 109 CDI, 111 CDI and 115 CDI Viano models (2003-2010)
items.cta: Compare rebuilt OM646 engine prices for your Mercedes Viano

items.question: Is the Mercedes Viano OM651 engine reliable?
items.answer: Generally well-engineered and capable of high mileage (300k+), but OM651 timing chain stretch and fuel pump failures are known weaknesses, particularly on 113 CDI and 116 CDI models.
keyPoints: Timing chain stretch is the most common replacement driver - engine-out job
Fuel pump can fail without warning (TSB 07.01.15 covers this issue)
Oil cooler seals degrade on higher-mileage examples
Many OM651 Vianos exceed 200k miles with proper maintenance
Post-2013 examples have improved chain components
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Viano OM651 2.2 CDI replacement engines

items.question: What engine problems does the Mercedes Viano 3.0 CDI V6 (OM642) have?
items.answer: The OM642 is a strong engine, but oil cooler seal failure is almost guaranteed between 70k-150k miles. The seals cost under £10 - accessing them requires 8-12 hours of labour.
keyPoints: Oil cooler sits in the engine Vee - seals degrade, causing oil leaks down the block
Turbo actuator carbon sticking - common on high-mileage fleet Vianos
EGR cooler cracking under thermal stress
The engine itself is durable once seals are addressed
Found exclusively in facelift Viano 3.0 CDI models (2010-2014)
items.cta: Compare rebuilt OM642 engine prices for your Viano 3.0 CDI

items.question: How many miles will a Mercedes Viano diesel engine last?
items.answer: Well-maintained OM646 and OM651 Viano diesels regularly exceed 250,000-300,000 miles. Multiple owner-documented examples exist beyond 500,000 miles on original bottom ends.
keyPoints: 2.2 CDI (OM646/OM651) is capable of very high mileage with proper care
Regular oil changes (every 10k-12k miles, not 20k) dramatically extends life
Timing chain/tensioner replacement at 120k-150k miles is good preventative practice
Injector pump seal failure (OM611/OM646) is the main early killer on neglected examples
Many W639 Vianos in taxi/private hire service exceed 200k-250k miles
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes Viano high-mileage diesel replacement engines

items.question: How much does a Mercedes Viano engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Used OM646 engines run £500-£1,400, rebuilt units with upgraded tensioners cost £1,800-£2,800 fitted. OM651 rebuilds average £1,900-£3,000. OM642 V6 diesels are £2,200-£3,500.
keyPoints: Supply only prices are lower - add £400-£800 for specialist fitting
OM646 timing chain failure caught early can be repaired (£768-£1,280)
Full replacement on a damaged OM646 typically runs £1,800-£2,800 supply & fit
OM651 replacement is more expensive due to complex timing chain labour
V6 petrol M272 balance shaft failure often exceeds vehicle value to repair
items.cta: Compare Mercedes Viano engine replacement prices from UK specialists

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Mercedes Viano?
items.answer: On a sound W639 Viano (2003-2014) with a good body and gearbox, replacement is almost always worthwhile. W638 (1996-2003) values are lower - weigh carefully against a £1,400-£2,800 replacement cost.
keyPoints: Good W639 Vianos still command £4,000-£8,000 - replacement makes strong financial sense
A rebuilt engine with upgraded components often outlasts the original
Used engines from breakers offer a lower-cost path (£600-£1,400 supply only)
W638 Vianos are now 20+ years old - replacement may exceed vehicle value
Private hire operators almost always replace - downtime cost is the bigger factor
items.cta: Check Viano engine replacement value - get quotes before you decide

items.question: Where is the Mercedes Viano engine code located?
items.answer: On OM646/OM651 engines, the engine code is stamped on the cylinder block near the starter motor, also visible on the timing cover sticker and drivers door pillar. For OM611, check the injection pump mounting area.
keyPoints: Most common location: flat machined pad on cylinder block near starter motor
Timing cover has a sticker with the full engine code on OM646/OM651
Vehicle registration lookup (our tool above) identifies your engine automatically
Check the service book - the original engine code is recorded at delivery
VIN decoder tools can also identify factory-fitted engine code
items.cta: Enter your reg above - we'll identify your Viano engine code instantly

items.question: What is the difference between a reconditioned and rebuilt Mercedes Viano engine?
items.answer: Reconditioned engines are stripped, cleaned and rebuilt to factory tolerances with new pistons, rings, bearings and seals. Rebuilt engines replace failed components (timing chain, gaskets, tensioner) but retain serviceable original hard parts.
keyPoints: Reconditioned: closest to new - every wearable component is replaced or machined
Rebuilt: cost-effective balance - fixes known failures without full component replacement
Both include new gaskets, seals, timing components and a 12-month warranty
Rebuilt is usually sufficient for OM646 timing chain failure repairs
Reconditioned is preferred for OM651 where chain stretch has occurred
items.cta: Compare reconditioned and rebuilt Viano engine prices side by side

items.question: What causes white smoke from a Mercedes Viano diesel on cold start?
items.answer: 30-60 seconds of white smoke on cold start on OM646/OM651 Vianos is usually glow plug or glow plug relay failure. If smoke continues once warm, suspect injector or timing issues.
keyPoints: Glow plug relay (OEM A0035451107) failure is very common on OM646
Faulty glow plugs cause incomplete combustion until cylinders warm up
White smoke that clears once warm - glow system is almost certainly the cause
White smoke that persists or smells of diesel - injector or timing problem
Owner-documented: 208k mile OM651 Viano with white smoke on cold start
items.cta: Diagnose your Viano cold start issue - compare repair quotes

items.question: Which Mercedes Viano engine is most reliable - diesel or petrol?
items.answer: The OM651 2.2 CDI (2010-2014) is the most durable when maintained, though timing chain stretch is a known cost. Petrol V6 engines (M112/M272) are smoother but suffer oil leaks and balance shaft failures on pre-2008 units.
keyPoints: OM651: modern, efficient, capable of 300k+ miles but chain stretch is expensive
OM646: well-proven but tensioner failure is predictable between 80k-140k miles
M112 V6 (3.2/3.7): smooth but heavy on fuel and prone to oil leaks
M272 V6 (3.5): avoid pre-2008 units - balance shaft failure is catastrophic
Diesel is the UK market favourite - easier parts access and specialist support
items.cta: Get quotes for the most reliable Viano engine for your needs
